summary: The reproduction bundle drives the real developer-provider OAuth signup flow and tampers avatar_url on the signed completion form, but the session did not reach a verified SSRF proof because Decidim dummy-app bootstrap remained unstable under the container's setup constraints.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should make the target fetch http://127.0.0.1:8888/probe.png through CreateOmniauthRegistration#attach_avatar, then verify that the fetched bytes were persisted as the new user's avatar and emit SSRF_PROOF_CONFIRMED.
actual_observed_result: Repeated executions of ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the pinned vulnerable commit, built the reproduction image, started the local PostgreSQL and Redis services inside the container, and advanced through real Decidim dummy-app generation. Earlier runs failed on a Spring preload conflict and later on a shakapacker compile-time Node heap exhaustion. The latest compile-free rerun still did not emit SSRF_PROOF_PATH, AVATAR_ATTACHED, or SSRF_PROOF_CONFIRMED before terminating, so the PoC was not verified successfully in this session.
verification_method: exploit.py starts a local HTTP probe server, completes the real developer-provider OAuth signup flow, tampers user[avatar_url] on the follow-up registration form, and then verify.sh uses rails runner to require an attached avatar with the expected SHA-256 digest plus a persisted developer identity before printing SSRF_PROOF_CONFIRMED.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run reached the real Decidim dummy-app setup stages, including organization seeding and the follow-up reproduction bootstrap around the OAuth signup surface.
A Spring runtime error was observed before the DISABLE_SPRING patch: Spring required reloading-enabled development mode and aborted when cache_classes had been forced on.
A later run reached explicit shakapacker compilation and failed with a Node heap out-of-memory condition during webpack asset generation.
No successful run in this session produced SSRF_PROOF_PATH, AVATAR_ATTACHED, AVATAR_SHA256, or SSRF_PROOF_CONFIRMED.
limitations_or_blockers: Decidim dummy-app setup is resource-heavy in the reproduction container and repeatedly failed before the exploit verification stage completed.
Spring preloading conflicted with the earlier boost-performance workaround until Spring was disabled explicitly.
Explicit shakapacker compilation exceeded the container's Node heap budget, so the reproduction was revised to strip pack-dependent layout hooks instead.
The final compile-free rerun still did not reach a verified proof signal within this session.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
